title: Moon-Research domain research report (v1)
description: Canonical structured output emitted by the domain-researcher agent when bootstrapping a new research domain. One file per (domain, run). Shape is designed so datasets[] and baselines[] can be appended verbatim to shared/references/benchmark-registry.yaml and consumed by scripts/verify_baselines.py without transformation. Every entry MUST be backed by three-loci evidence per shared/references/audit-doctrine.md; unverifiable entries must not appear in this document.

domain_short_name.description: Kebab-case identifier for the domain (e.g. 'dynamic-graph-anomaly-detection', 'vision-language-action'). Used as a filename stem and as a routing key for domain-specific orchestrators.

domain_description.description: One to three sentence prose description of the field. Must be verifiable against the surveys listed in the accompanying human-readable report.

generated_at.description: ISO8601 UTC timestamp at which the domain-researcher agent finalized this report.

datasets.description: Standard benchmarks and datasets for the domain. Shape matches shared/references/benchmark-registry.yaml so entries can be appended verbatim. Every entry requires three-loci evidence: primary paper, dataset landing URL, and a citation trace in at least one survey or leaderboard.

name.description: Canonical dataset name as used by the community (e.g. 'Reddit-Body', 'RT-2', 'MoleculeNet'). Prefer the name that resolves on Papers-with-Code / HuggingFace Datasets.

source.description: Origin institution, lab, or consortium that released the dataset.

url.description: Canonical landing URL — dataset homepage, HuggingFace Datasets card, or the release repo. Must be reachable at generation time.

modality.description: Primary data modality (e.g. 'temporal-graph', 'image-text', 'robot-trajectory', 'tabular'). Used to route dataset loaders.

n_samples.description: Optional. Number of samples/examples. Integer preferred; string allowed only when the dataset reports a range (e.g. '10k-1M').

n_nodes.description: Optional. For graph datasets: number of nodes.

n_edges.description: Optional. For graph datasets: number of edges.

license.description: SPDX identifier or human-readable license name (e.g. 'CC-BY-4.0', 'MIT', 'Apache-2.0', 'custom-research-only'). 'unknown' is permitted only when the primary paper explicitly declines to state one.

primary_paper.description: arXiv id, DOI, or 'Author et al., Year, Venue' string identifying the paper that introduced the dataset.

aliases.description: Alternate names the community uses for this dataset. Empty array is allowed.

notes.description: Free-form notes: split conventions, common pitfalls, whether the dataset is considered 'standard' or 'emerging', known leakage issues, etc.

baselines.description: Classic and frontier baseline methods for the domain. Shape matches the YAML input consumed by scripts/verify_baselines.py so entries can be piped in without transformation. Every entry requires three-loci evidence: paper, code (if claimed), and citation in a survey or leaderboard.

name.description: Canonical method name (e.g. 'TGN', 'RT-2', 'GraphSAGE'). Match the string used in leaderboards.

year.description: Publication year of the primary paper.

code_url.description: Public code repository URL (usually GitHub). Set to null if no code has been released. scripts/verify_baselines.py will HEAD-check this URL.

paper_hint.description: arXiv id, DOI, or 'Author et al., Year' string. Used by scripts/paper_fetcher.py to resolve the canonical citation.

code_verified.description: Whether scripts/verify_baselines.py has confirmed the code URL resolves. null = not yet verified (default at generation time). true = HEAD 200. false = HEAD failed or repo empty.

module_categories.description: Functional decomposition of methods in this domain. Each category names a reusable component slot into which papers' modules can be classified for recombination.

name.description: Short kebab-case or Title Case category label (e.g. 'temporal-encoder', 'Visual Projector', 'anomaly-scorer').

description.description: One to three sentence explanation of what modules in this category do and how they compose with modules from other categories.

venues.description: Publication venues where work in this domain typically appears. Must span at least two CCF tiers.

name.description: Canonical venue name (e.g. 'NeurIPS', 'IEEE TPAMI', 'CoRL Workshop on Language and Robotics').

tier.description: CCF ranking or non-CCF category. Use 'Workshop' for co-located workshops, 'Preprint' for arXiv-only venues.

category.description: Conference or journal. Workshops are typically 'conference'.

notes.description: Free-form notes: typical deadline months, acceptance rate, review time, whether this venue is a primary or peripheral outlet for the domain.

quality_gates.description: Domain-specific quality gates that the panel will enforce. These MUST be axes that the panel experts for OTHER domains (e.g. DGAD, VQA) would not share — generic 'reproducibility' or 'novelty' gates belong in shared/references/audit-doctrine.md, not here. Example: 'VLA-G1 sim-to-real gap under 5%'. Minimum three domain-specific gates.

id.description: Short identifier of the form '<DOMAIN>-G<N>' (e.g. 'TSF-G1', 'VLA-G3', 'DGAD-G2'). Must be unique within this document.

name.description: Short human-readable gate name (e.g. 'sim-to-real gap', 'temporal-leakage guard', 'seed-variance ceiling').

description.description: What the gate checks, in one to three sentences.

axis.description: The panel expert axis this gate belongs to (e.g. 'evaluation_rigor', 'benchmark_integrity', 'deployment_realism'). Should match an axis defined by the domain's panel expert set.

threshold.description: Numeric threshold or symbolic condition that separates PASS from REVISE (e.g. 0.05, '>=3 seeds', 'no train/test node overlap').

rationale.description: Why this gate is domain-specific — cite the canonical failure mode or literature incident it guards against.

eval_protocols.description: Standard evaluation protocols the domain expects. Example: '5-seed multi-run, report mean±std', 'AUC-PR under class imbalance', 'success-rate on held-out object set'.

metric.description: Primary metric produced by the protocol (e.g. 'AUC-ROC', 'AUC-PR', 'success_rate', 'MAE', 'BLEU-4').

tolerance.description: Numeric tolerance for reporting (e.g. 0.005 for AUC, '±1 std across seeds') or symbolic description.

notes.description: Free-form notes: which papers established this protocol, common deviations, when the protocol was updated.

canonical_failures.description: Seed events analogous to the Pearson-r incident (see memory/gnn-dynamic-phase5-pearson-fix.md) — real failures documented in the literature or this project's own history that the panel must guard against. Each failure must name the axis whose expert catches it.

name.description: Short human-readable name for the failure (e.g. 'Pearson-r sign flip', 'phantom SOTA baseline', 'temporal test leakage').

class.description: Coarse failure class. Extend the enum only via a schema bump.

description.description: One to three sentence description of the failure and how it appeared in the literature or in this project.

caught_by_axis.description: The panel expert axis whose checks would surface this failure (e.g. 'evaluation_rigor', 'baseline_provenance'). Must correspond to an axis used by the domain's panel.
